characters.physical_details: Pregnant until chapter 17; postpartum tear/bleeding and pain in chapter 18; callused hands.
characters.literacy_languages: English reading/writing; keeps debt and ration notes.

state_transitions.change: Learns pram arrears (GBP 12 8s) include forged authorization in her name.
Refuses ring pawn/false statement; Tom leaves home.
Flood labor: delivers daughter and signs witnessed emergency guardian declaration naming Noreen.
Sets Tom's contact/payment terms, takes house key, and starts weekly arrears payment.

characters.availability: Present all chapters; postpartum at home in chapter 18.

characters.physical_details: Severe edema and preeclampsia signs pre-birth; postpartum weakness after hemorrhage.
characters.occupation_status: No stable wages; Saint Brigid resident from chapter 16.
characters.literacy_languages: Fluent English; uses forms, cards, and written lists.

state_transitions.change: Red-flag preeclampsia (166/108) with no-delay protocol.
Preterm daughter delivered (~34 weeks); infant transferred; maternal hemorrhage survives.
Moves to Saint Brigid emergency lodging after custody/reputation threats.
Gets conditional nursery card and nominates Elsie as support person.

characters.physical_details: Smoker's cough; alcohol odor; volatile under debt stress.
characters.occupation_status: Depot conductor; suspended after chapter 13; part rota by chapter 18.
characters.literacy_languages: Functional English for forms and betting slips.

state_transitions.change: Admits gambling-linked depot theft and leaves household.
Absent/unreachable during Elsie's labor.
Returns, reports about GBP 90 debt, signs terms, and surrenders house key.

characters.availability: Unreliable after chapter 13; limited return in chapter 18.

characters.physical_details: Wire-rim clerk profile; distal radius fracture and cast in chapter 11.

key_relationships.noreen_walsh: husband using money, paperwork, and reputation control
key_relationships.arthur_hale: billeted witness he tries to manage

characters.occupation_status: Municipal accounts clerk; document and process control in household.

state_transitions.change: Falls on ice; wrist fractured; casted about 6-7 weeks.
Finds intimacy letter and escalates fitness/custody threats.
Signs maintenance/conditional-access paperwork while preserving legal pressure.

characters.availability: Present most chapters; absent during key chapter 14 emergency window.

characters.physical_details: Veteran district midwife; crisis triage and documentation discipline.
characters.occupation_status: NHS district midwife with escalation and safeguarding authority.

state_transitions.change: Records preeclampsia trajectory and emergency signals.
Leads preterm home delivery and hemorrhage stabilization.
Secures Saint Brigid transfer and intake paperwork.

characters.availability: On-call throughout; central in chapters 12, 14, 16, 17, and 18.

characters.physical_details: Lean linesman with frost-cracked hands; practical emergency demeanor.
characters.occupation_status: Electricity board linesman; billeted chapter 7 to early chapter 18.

state_transitions.change: Billeted into Walsh house by council emergency order.
Provides heat/communications/logistics during labor emergency.
Billet order lifted; reassigned to high-line work outside Barrow Lane.

characters.availability: Present chapters 7 to early 18, then departs.

timeline.story_start: Monday before Christmas 1962, at freeze onset on Barrow Lane.
timeline.estimated_span: About 14 weeks, ending in early spring thaw/flood.

seasonal_track.season: Deep freeze: false labor, outages, ration strain, secrecy escalation.
Late-freeze crisis: injury, red-flag pregnancy, preterm emergency birth.
Thaw/flood: gatekeeping, separations, negotiated spring terms.

key_events.event: Arthur Hale billeted into Walsh house.
Tom confesses theft tied to gambling debt and leaves.
Noreen preterm labor, infant transfer, and maternal hemorrhage.
Peter weaponizes letter; Noreen moved to Saint Brigid.
Elsie flood birth and guardian declaration naming Noreen.
Conditional nursery access and formal separation/payment terms set.

geography.primary_setting: Barrow Lane terraces, Calderbank, West Riding, England.

key_locations.name: Harrow House
key_locations.details: Flood-prone terrace; chapter 17 birth site and chapter 18 negotiation site.

key_locations.name: Walsh House
key_locations.details: Adjacent terrace; billeting and control-conflict center.

key_locations.name: Mrs Bhatti's Corner Shop
key_locations.details: Credit line, phone relay, and witness/information hub.

key_locations.name: Calderbank Maternity Clinic and District Midwife Network
key_locations.details: Risk monitoring, escalation, and formal obstetric paperwork.

key_locations.name: Calderbank Infirmary and County Special Care Nursery
key_locations.details: Maternal trauma care and neonatal transfer destination.

key_locations.name: Saint Brigid House
key_locations.details: Church emergency lodging with curfew and conditional nursery compliance rules.

distances: Harrow and Walsh homes share a wall; sound carries directly.
Short local routes become hazardous or impassable in freeze/flood.
Clinic/infirmary access depends on unstable buses, ad hoc vans, or foot travel.
Saint Brigid access depends on transport timing and curfew windows.

world_rules: Weather disruption continuously affects transport, fuel, and medical access.
Coal/paraffin scarcity drives household power and risk decisions.
Medical systems exist but weather can force dangerous home management.
Paperwork and institutional rules gate infant access and maternal movement.
Same-sex intimacy carries major custody/reputation risk in this setting.

power_structure.mechanism: Cash/key/phone control, formal messaging, custody and reputation threats.
Debt concealment, forged signatures, and prior ration-key control.
Bookmaker deadlines, runner visits, doorstep pressure.
Clinical triage authority, records, and transfer referrals.
Curfew compliance, residency conditions, and conditional nursery permissions.

objects.name: Pram arrears schedule and receipts
objects.status: GBP 12 8s outstanding; GBP 1 paid in chapter 18; GBP 1 due each Thursday by 16:00

objects.name: Bookmaker and forged-withdrawal packet
objects.origin: Hayes/Cragg slips and forged withdrawal dockets

objects.name: Infant transfer bracelet
objects.status: Retained as proof during separation from infant

objects.name: Emergency guardian declaration
objects.status: Filed at clinic; names Noreen for emergency consent

objects.name: Conditional nursery attendance card
objects.status: Active if curfew and sign-in rules are met

objects.name: Harrow front-door key
objects.origin: Surrendered by Tom under chapter 18 terms
objects.status: Stored in tea tin; Tom has no resident key access

debts.deadline: GBP 1 each Thursday by 16:00 after chapter 18 goodwill payment
debts.status: Active; escalation risk if installments are missed

debts.amount: About GBP 147 at chapter 13 peak; about GBP 90 plus interest by chapter 18
GBP 14 10s plus about GBP 6 float shortfall

income_sources.source: Maintenance postal order to Saint Brigid

knowledge_state.knows: Tom forged Elsie's signatures and tied her name to debt papers.
Tom's gambling debt, bookmaker pressure, and depot theft are linked.
Elsie and Noreen intimacy details and weaponizes them for custody/reputation leverage.
Noreen's medical risk is worsened by domestic/transport obstruction and records this formally.
Elsie's guardian declaration naming her was logged at clinic.
Nursery access depends on Saint Brigid compliance, curfew/sign-in, and probation behavior.

environmental_constants: Interiors repeatedly smell of coal smoke, paraffin, damp wool, bleach, and stale milk.
Shared-wall acoustics remain continuity-critical for secrecy, warnings, and evidence.
The world shifts from freeze to thaw-flood with persistent leaks, mud, and unstable transport.
